ab: ne mo’una’a oko mosola yone tfuya’o emoomia, mo’ucula tmopsʉ, cu noezuhu ne maibayu, momeacula nochʉmʉ ne’e.
ch: 小時候我們家住在特富野，上學以後，就搬到了嘉義市，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in tfuya’o. After I started school, we moved to Chiayi and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: mo’ucula tmopsʉ to taingakoo, mi’o tmopsʉ ho aʉsvʉtʉ to ceoceona no h’oyunsova ta cou, peela cohivi mo a’ʉmtʉ mav’ov’o ’o h’oyunsova ta cou ta taivang.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: ko’koeno, ieni homo tosvo ta tonsoha maitan’e, mimza yuyuso ’o ohaevano’u ahocngʉ ho maine’e to huehungumia ho bobaito to mamameoi, ocia cohivi na ceoceona no h’oyunsova ta cou.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: micu’so tonvoevʉhʉ ’o mameoi, at’inghi mioc’on’aso mais’a haah’o ho isi aiti.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: os’o tuocosi cuma na osko peela ci e’e, mita na’no empompohsu ho mita bua cou, mita maezo meelʉ bua maaya, bua puutu ho bua sbukunu.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的鄒語說得很流利，也會說日語、漢語和布農語。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in cou, as well as Japanese, Chinese and Bunun.

ab: i’mi ta hia’u tuucocosi ho cohivi, ’iama mita ahoi ho mohtan’ala oko ho aʉsvʉtʉ bua cou ho bua maaya zou sbukunu ’o vcongʉsi.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候學鄒語和日本話，太太是布農人。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Bunun, and he knows many Chinese at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: ne miza maica yupepeahngʉyʉ, ’e mameoi mo eʉsvʉsvʉtʉ tomo man’i ci tela hia e’ohʉ ho ea fou ci a’a’ausna.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: e’vono o’a imza peela a’sʉftona acʉha ta’hongi, at’inghi mimza boemi to souci ho loku’in, tenamiala ivaha ʉmnʉ ci ta’lʉi.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: ahoi ne mo’un’a oko ho o’a o’ula cohivi na iachi ceoceona no h’oyunsova ta cou, a’ʉmtʉs’a iachi pa’hikokoe’i.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: fihta hia ausuhcu man’i na isi cohivi, cohivi ho cou ’e iachi, mo na’no h’unasi ci cou a’ʉmta atva’esa itanveni ci a’a’ausna.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為鄒族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a cou is unique and something to be proud of.
